pages.raw_text:
Notwithstanding the unpleasant
weather there was a good attend
ance at the meeting of the Central
Labor union last night. The Boiler
makers union sent credentials and
the delegates were seated. This is
the first time the Boilermakers
have been represented in the new
Central and brings up the total of
unions affiliated now to 20. The
Structural Trades Alliance and the
Central Labor Union have been act
ing in perfect accord, so that all
local unions are acting together.


The Structural Trades Alliance
sent communication TO the Cen
trail labor union which was read
last night, in which the necessity
of placing heavy fines on members
of unions patronizing Natatorium
Park or lines of the Washington
Water Power company when with
in walking distance or when it is
possible to secure other means of
transportation. 1t was explained
that a large number of local unions
had already a law covering this
some question. The local board of
business agents asked that unions
affiliated with the Central union
take steps to properly picket Nata-
forum Park and the suggestions
was approved by the central body.
The Electrical Workers present
said that it was necessary to keep
the presence of pickets at the park
unknown to the company, as they
would be ordered off the grounds.


The Bakers union is having trou-
bic in having their scale accepted
by the Golden West Bakery, which
is located at Union Park. 1t was
reported to the Central union some
time ago that all the bakeries had
been signed up, but this proved to
be untrue, at least in this one in
stance.


The settlement of the Tefft and
Schaffer trouble still hangs fire.
There is, however, disposition on
both sides to settle the trouble.


The molderss troubles with the
National Iron Works came up again
last night, but the matter went
over to another meeting. There is
a desire upon the part of the local
unions to avoid, if possible, a rup-
